source_formulae: P0R02967: BIBO stability (statement and proof obligation).
P0R02970: (ii) small-gain condition on the fast/slow interconnection, and
P0R02971: (iii) bounded inputs (disturbances, modelling error), the closed loop is BIBO: every state/output of interest remains bounded.

P0R02972: Certificate sketch. Construct a composite Lyapunov function V_total = V_fast + V_slow + cross_term, with V_fast the MS-QEC/coherence Lyapunov (uses your existing V 0 under bounded noise), V_slow a quasicritical band Lyapunov (see below), > 0 small. For sufficiently separated timescales and scheduled gains, V_total _f V_fast _s V_slow + input, with _f, _s > 0 and finite. This yields BIBO via standard comparison lemmas; the "bounded noise descent" hooks already present satisfy the required inequalities.

source_formulae: P0R02974: Pick a band function measuring deviation from quasicriticality and phase coherence, e.g., V_slow = (sigma1) + (R R*), with R an order parameter and R* its set-point for manoeuvrability. With the existing stabiliser form, there exist {, G_s} such that V_slow c_s V_slow + noise (layer-wise constants), which is the slow-channel certificate. This is consistent with your earlier stabiliser and MI/R arguments.
